edges.description: Loss of CD27 costimulation weakens the antiviral CD8 T-cell response to EBV.
Loss of CD27 signaling also weakens NK and iNKT-cell mediated antiviral surveillance.
CD27 loss also disrupts helper-dependent B-cell maturation and antibody generation.
Concurrent impairment of T-cell antiviral control and T cell-dependent humoral immunity manifests as combined immunodeficiency.
Reduced innate cytotoxic control contributes to incomplete containment of EBV infection.
Weakened NK and iNKT-cell surveillance further permits EBV-driven immune pathology.
Failure of EBV-specific cytotoxic immunity permits sustained viral replication.
Persistent viral burden promotes uncontrolled EBV-associated immune pathology.
Impaired B-cell maturation lowers serum immunoglobulin levels.
Weak antibody-mediated immunity increases susceptibility to repeated infection.
Uncontrolled EBV-driven B-cell expansion manifests clinically as lymphoproliferative disorder.
Hyperinflammatory immune activation can culminate in HLH-like hemophagocytosis.
Chronic EBV-driven lymphoid proliferation increases risk of malignant lymphoma.
EBV-driven immune dysregulation and hyperinflammation can present as ocular autoinflammation.
Systemic autoinflammation downstream of CD27/CD70 immune dysregulation can involve joints.
Hyperinflammatory immune activation manifests clinically as recurrent or periodic fever.

nodes.description: Allogeneic HSCT is the main potentially curative treatment for severe CD27-related disease, especially when lymphoma, HLH, or refractory EBV-driven immune dysregulation develops.

evidence.reference_title: Extended clinical and immunological phenotype and transplant outcome in CD27 and CD70 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Nineteen patients underwent allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) prior to adulthood predominantly because of lymphoma, with 95% survival without disease recurrence.

evidence.reference_title: Combined immunodeficiency with life-threatening EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disorder in patients lacking functional CD27.
evidence.snippet: Two of 8 patients died, 2 others underwent allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation successfully, and one received anti-CD20 (rituximab) therapy repeatedly.

nodes.description: Arthritis occurs as part of the autoinflammatory phenotype reported in a substantial subset of patients with CD27 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Extended clinical and immunological phenotype and transplant outcome in CD27 and CD70 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Twenty-one patients (43%) developed autoinflammatory features including uveitis, arthritis, and periodic fever.

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations in TNFRSF7/CD27: Clinical, immunologic, and genetic characterization of human CD27 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: 4 novel mutations were identified: homozygous missense c.G287A/p.C96Y

evidence.reference_title: Combined immunodeficiency with life-threatening EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disorder in patients lacking functional CD27.
evidence.snippet: We report the simultaneous confirmation of human CD27 deficiency in 3 independent families (8 patients) due to a homozygous mutation (p. Cys53Tyr) revealed by whole exome sequencing, leading to disruption of an evolutionarily conserved cystein knot motif of the transmembrane receptor.

nodes.description: CD27 deficiency presents as a combined immunodeficiency with both T-cell and B-cell dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: Combined immunodeficiency with life-threatening EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disorder in patients lacking functional CD27.
evidence.snippet: Since homozygosity mapping and exome sequencing did not reveal additional modifying factors, our findings suggest that lack of functional CD27 predisposes towards a combined immunodeficiency associated with potentially fatal EBV-driven hemo-phagocytosis, lymphoproliferation, and lymphoma development.

nodes.description: Longitudinal follow-up should monitor EBV burden, lymphadenopathy, cytopenias, systemic inflammatory features, and symptoms concerning for lymphoproliferative disease or lymphoma.

evidence.reference_title: Extended clinical and immunological phenotype and transplant outcome in CD27 and CD70 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The majority of patients (90%) were EBV+ at diagnosis, but only ∼30% presented with infectious mononucleosis. Lymphoproliferation and lymphoma were the main clinical manifestations (70% and 43%, respectively)

nodes.description: Loss of CD27-mediated immune surveillance leaves EBV insufficiently controlled, predisposing to lymphoproliferative disease, HLH-like hyperinflammation, and malignant lymphoma.

evidence.reference_title: Combined immunodeficiency with life-threatening EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disorder in patients lacking functional CD27.
evidence.snippet: Since homozygosity mapping and exome sequencing did not reveal additional modifying factors, our findings suggest that lack of functional CD27 predisposes towards a combined immunodeficiency associated with potentially fatal EBV-driven hemo-phagocytosis, lymphoproliferation, and lymphoma development.

evidence.reference_title: Extended clinical and immunological phenotype and transplant outcome in CD27 and CD70 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Lymphoproliferation and lymphoma were the main clinical manifestations (70% and 43%, respectively), and 9 of the CD27-deficient patients developed HLH.

nodes.description: Suspected HLH, EBV-driven lymphoproliferative disease, or lymphoma requires urgent hematology/oncology-directed treatment using disease-standard protocols, with infection support and consideration of HSCT once acute disease is controlled.

evidence.reference_title: Extended clinical and immunological phenotype and transplant outcome in CD27 and CD70 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Lymphoproliferation and lymphoma were the main clinical manifestations (70% and 43%, respectively), and 9 of the CD27-deficient patients developed HLH.

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations in TNFRSF7/CD27: Clinical, immunologic, and genetic characterization of human CD27 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disease/hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis, Hodgkin lymphoma, uveitis, and recurrent infections were the predominant clinical features.

nodes.description: Some patients develop HLH-like hemophagocytic hyperinflammation during uncontrolled EBV disease.

evidence.reference_title: Combined immunodeficiency with life-threatening EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disorder in patients lacking functional CD27.
evidence.snippet: Phenotypes varied from asymptomatic memory B-cell deficiency (n=3) to EBV-associated hemophagocytosis and lymphoproliferative disorder (LPD; n=3) and malignant lymphoma (n=2; +1 after LPD).

nodes.description: Reduced circulating immunoglobulin levels emerge in many affected individuals, often after EBV infection.

evidence.reference_title: CD27 deficiency is associated with combined immunodeficiency and persistent symptomatic EBV viremia.
evidence.snippet: The index patient was hypogammaglobulinemic, and immunoglobulin replacement therapy was initiated.

evidence.reference_title: Combined immunodeficiency with life-threatening EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disorder in patients lacking functional CD27.
evidence.snippet: Following EBV infection, hypogammaglobulinemia developed in at least 3 of the affected individuals, while specific anti-viral and anti-polysaccharide antibodies and EBV-specific T-cell responses were detectable.

nodes.description: Immunoglobulin replacement is supportive treatment for the antibody deficiency component of CD27 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: CD27 deficiency is associated with combined immunodeficiency and persistent symptomatic EBV viremia.
evidence.snippet: The index patient was hypogammaglobulinemic, and immunoglobulin replacement therapy was initiated.

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations in TNFRSF7/CD27: Clinical, immunologic, and genetic characterization of human CD27 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Immunoglobulin substitution therapy was administered in 5 of the newly diagnosed cases.

nodes.description: CD27 deficiency compromises expansion and effector differentiation of protective EBV-specific CD8-positive T cells, especially lytic antigen responses, allowing persistent EBV infection and defective clearance of EBV-transformed B cells.

evidence.reference_title: Extended clinical and immunological phenotype and transplant outcome in CD27 and CD70 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Detailed immunological characterization revealed aberrant generation of memory B and T cells, including a paucity of EBV-specific T cells, and impaired effector function of CD8+ T cells, thereby providing mechanistic insight into cellular defects underpinning the clinical features of disrupted CD27/CD70 signaling.

evidence.reference_title: CD27 is required for protective lytic EBV antigen-specific CD8+ T-cell expansion.
evidence.snippet: While overall CD8+ T-cell expansion and composition are unaltered after antibody blocking of CD27, only some EBV-specific CD8+ T-cell responses, exemplified by early lytic EBV antigen BMLF1-specific CD8+ T cells, are inhibited in their proliferation and killing of EBV-transformed B cells.

nodes.description: CD27 deficiency impairs T cell-dependent B-cell responses, memory B-cell generation, and antibody production. This produces hypogammaglobulinemia and contributes to recurrent infection susceptibility.

evidence.reference_title: CD27 deficiency is associated with combined immunodeficiency and persistent symptomatic EBV viremia.
evidence.snippet: Immunologically, lack of CD27 expression was associated with impaired T cell-dependent B-cell responses and T-cell dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: Combined immunodeficiency with life-threatening EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disorder in patients lacking functional CD27.
evidence.snippet: Phenotypes varied from asymptomatic memory B-cell deficiency (n=3) to EBV-associated hemophagocytosis and lymphoproliferative disorder (LPD; n=3) and malignant lymphoma (n=2; +1 after LPD).

evidence.reference_title: Combined immunodeficiency with life-threatening EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disorder in patients lacking functional CD27.
evidence.snippet: Following EBV infection, hypogammaglobulinemia developed in at least 3 of the affected individuals, while specific anti-viral and anti-polysaccharide antibodies and EBV-specific T-cell responses were detectable.

nodes.description: Biallelic CD27/TNFRSF7 variants reduce or abolish cell-surface CD27 and disrupt CD27-CD70 signaling. This removes a key TNF-receptor-family costimulatory input required for effective adaptive antiviral responses.

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations in TNFRSF7/CD27: Clinical, immunologic, and genetic characterization of human CD27 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Expression of cell-surface and soluble CD27 was significantly reduced in patients and heterozygous family members.

evidence.reference_title: CD27 is required for protective lytic EBV antigen-specific CD8+ T-cell expansion.
evidence.snippet: This suggests that CD27 is not required for all CD8+ T-cell expansions and cytotoxicity but is required for a subset of CD8+ T-cell responses that protect us from EBV pathology.

nodes.description: CD27 deficiency markedly predisposes to EBV-driven malignant lymphoma, including Hodgkin lymphoma.

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations in TNFRSF7/CD27: Clinical, immunologic, and genetic characterization of human CD27 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disease/hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis, Hodgkin lymphoma, uveitis, and recurrent infections were the predominant clinical features.

evidence.reference_title: Extended clinical and immunological phenotype and transplant outcome in CD27 and CD70 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Our data highlight the marked predisposition to lymphoma of both CD27- and CD70-deficient patients.

nodes.description: EBV-associated nonmalignant lymphoproliferation is a core manifestation of CD27 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Combined immunodeficiency with life-threatening EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disorder in patients lacking functional CD27.
evidence.snippet: Phenotypes varied from asymptomatic memory B-cell deficiency (n=3) to EBV-associated hemophagocytosis and lymphoproliferative disorder (LPD; n=3) and malignant lymphoma (n=2; +1 after LPD).

evidence.reference_title: Extended clinical and immunological phenotype and transplant outcome in CD27 and CD70 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Lymphoproliferation and lymphoma were the main clinical manifestations (70% and 43%, respectively), and 9 of the CD27-deficient patients developed HLH.

nodes.description: Persistent or chronic active Epstein-Barr virus infection is one of the most characteristic manifestations of CD27 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: CD27 deficiency is associated with combined immunodeficiency and persistent symptomatic EBV viremia.
evidence.snippet: Both patients had persistent symptomatic EBV viremia.

evidence.reference_title: Extended clinical and immunological phenotype and transplant outcome in CD27 and CD70 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The majority of patients (90%) were EBV+ at diagnosis

nodes.description: Periodic or recurrent fever is part of the autoinflammatory phenotype seen in CD27 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Extended clinical and immunological phenotype and transplant outcome in CD27 and CD70 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Twenty-one patients (43%) developed autoinflammatory features including uveitis, arthritis, and periodic fever.

nodes.description: Repeated infections occur on the background of combined immunodeficiency and defective humoral immunity.

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations in TNFRSF7/CD27: Clinical, immunologic, and genetic characterization of human CD27 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disease/hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis, Hodgkin lymphoma, uveitis, and recurrent infections were the predominant clinical features.

nodes.description: In severely affected CD27-deficient patients, innate cytotoxic lymphocyte compartments are also compromised, with reduced NK-cell function and fewer invariant NKT cells further weakening antiviral immune surveillance.

evidence.reference_title: Combined immunodeficiency with life-threatening EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disorder in patients lacking functional CD27.
evidence.snippet: In severely affected patients, numbers of iNKT cells and NK-cell function were reduced.

nodes.description: Anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody therapy has been used as adjunctive or bridge treatment for severe EBV-driven CD20-positive B-cell lymphoproliferation or lymphoma in CD27 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Combined immunodeficiency with life-threatening EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disorder in patients lacking functional CD27.
evidence.snippet: Two of 8 patients died, 2 others underwent allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation successfully, and one received anti-CD20 (rituximab) therapy repeatedly.

nodes.description: Uveitis is a recurrent inflammatory manifestation in CD27 deficiency and is part of the broader EBV-associated immune dysregulation phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations in TNFRSF7/CD27: Clinical, immunologic, and genetic characterization of human CD27 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: EBV-associated lymphoproliferative disease/hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis, Hodgkin lymphoma, uveitis, and recurrent infections were the predominant clinical features.

evidence.reference_title: Extended clinical and immunological phenotype and transplant outcome in CD27 and CD70 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Twenty-one patients (43%) developed autoinflammatory features including uveitis, arthritis, and periodic fever.

nodes.description: Vaccination decisions should be individualized with immunology and infectious-disease input. Non-live vaccines may be indicated, whereas live-attenuated vaccines should be deferred or avoided when clinically significant cellular or combined immunodeficiency is present.

evidence.reference_title: Altered Immunocompetence | Vaccines & Immunizations | CDC
evidence.snippet: Administration of live vaccines might need to be deferred until immune function has improved.

evidence.reference_title: Altered Immunocompetence | Vaccines & Immunizations | CDC
evidence.snippet: Persons with most forms of altered immunocompetence should not receive live vaccines (MMR, varicella, MMRV, LAIV, yellow fever, Ty21a oral typhoid, BCG, smallpox, and rotavirus).
